rationale: The cited article (moral licensing via self-concept in sequential choices) is conceptually adjacent to the citing paper’s focus on psychologically driven shifts toward riskier financial choice, so it could be a theory/background source. However, the evidence is sparse: there is only one DOI-level match and no in-text citation context, and the citing note’s stated contributions do not explicitly discuss licensing. Given this, the pair cannot be safely dropped but is not clearly strong enough to keep outright.
